mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly suppressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 3 months, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand across durable and non-durable sectors, which reduces near-term corporate revenue growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over individual series in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side bottlenecks or demand shocks that trigger risk-off sentiment, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not predict negative 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction when tested in a 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price index levels above 315 force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, which caps equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 315, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding economic activity and stronger corporate earnings potential, which supports higher equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth signals robust macroeconomic expansion that boosts corporate profit margins and equity demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during early-cycle expansions, or the predictive relationship reverses sign in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and payroll expansion reveals structural supply-demand imbalances that increase input costs and compress margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The interaction between INDPRO and PAYEMS growth rates shows no predictive power for 5-day returns, or the divergence metric fails to generate negative returns in > 55% of triggered cases.
